text: The remarkable performance of Transformers in natural language processing and computer vision in the recent years has motivated many researchers to apply them to time series analysis. Due to their unique abilities of learning long-term dependencies and complex patterns in data, many new Transformer-based architectures have been proposed, which motivates us to conduct a comprehensive survey on the recent advancements of Transformers for time series analysis from two aspects: 1) Network structure: We summarize the ways in which the basic structure of vanilla Transformers is modified to handle the characteristics of time series data, such as positional encoding and efficient attention, and 2) Application fields: Based on the tasks they are used for, we classify time series Transformers into forecasting, anomaly detection and classification, and discuss their applications and effectiveness. In addition, in order to validate the robustness of time series Transformers, we carry out a series of experiments which involves model size comparison, robustness evaluation of various designs, and validation of their ability to handle highly differing seasonal and trend components. Finally, our goal is to summarize the current researches and summarize the trends and future works of time series Transformers via our comprehensive survey.
headline: Fully Human Written
